channel.title: AI Artifacts on Christa Burger
channel.description: Recent content in AI Artifacts on Christa Burger

item.title: Turn AI Output Into Infrastructure
item.description:
The best AI outputs are not just deliverables.

They become infrastructure.

A meeting summary becomes a decision log.

A transcript becomes a content workflow.

A one-off checklist becomes a reusable control.

A draft becomes a communication template.

A messy brainstorm becomes a playbook.

That is where AI starts to compound.

Not when it helps create the thing.

When it helps create the thing that helps create future things.

That is leverage.

item.title: Chat Is the Workshop, Not the Warehouse
item.description:
A lot of AI value dies in chat windows.

Good idea. Useful framework. Strong draft. Solid analysis.

Then it disappears into the scroll swamp, where all productive conversations go to become vaguely remembered.

This is a problem.

If AI creates something valuable, it should often become an artifact outside the chat:

  • A document
  • A template
  • A report
  • A workflow
  • A decision log
  • A playbook

Chat is a great workshop. It is a terrible warehouse.

item.title: Good AI Should Leave You With Something Usable
item.description:
A good AI interaction should often leave behind something usable.

Not just an answer.

An artifact.

A checklist. A template. A decision log. A draft. A table. A playbook. A workflow. A reusable prompt. A meeting summary with owners.

The answer may help once.

The artifact helps again.

That is the difference between convenience and leverage.

I have become much more interested in asking:

“What should this become?”

A document? A template? A control? A process? A decision aid?
